  "findings": [
    "High-voltage insulating gloves are either of an incorrect class for the system voltage or have an expired test date stamp."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately.",
    "Acquire and inspect a pair of high-voltage gloves of the correct class with a valid, unexpired test date stamp.",
    "Perform a manual air-leak test on the compliant gloves before resuming work."
  ],
  "rationale": "Working on or near active high-voltage automotive systems with expired or incorrectly rated insulating gloves poses an immediate, critical risk of electrocution or severe arc flash injury. Work must be halted until compliant Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) is secured.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 70E Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.137 (Electrical Protective Equipment)",
    "OEM High-Voltage Safety Procedures"
  ]
}
